blocks:
Sūrat al-Naml (The Ants)

Makkan, and it is 93 verses

Sūrat al-Naml, Makkan

Its number of verses is ninety-three verses according to the Hijazi count, ninety-four according to the Basran and Shami counts, and ninety-three according to the Kufan count. Its points of difference are two verses: وَأُولُوا بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ ** (and those of great might) [counted by the] Hijazi [school], and مِنْ قَوارِيرَ ** (of crystal) [counted by] other than the Kufan [school].

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) طس (Tā Sīn) In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for طس ** (Tā Sīn), its meaning is: I am the Seeker (الطّالب), the All-Hearing (السّميع). (٢) تِلْكَ آياتُ الْقُرْآنِ وَكِتابٍ مُبِينٍ (These are the signs of the Quran and a manifest Book,) (٣) هُدىً وَبُشْرى لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (a guidance and a good news for the faithful,) (٤) الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ (who maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and are certain of the Hereafter.) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ زَيَّنَّا لَهُمْ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** by making them desirable to their natures and beloved to their souls, **فَهُمْ يَعْمَهُونَ ** (so they wander in bewilderment) concerning them, not realizing what follows them.

(٥) أُوْلئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ الْعَذابِ ** (5) They are the ones for whom there is a terrible punishment, such as killing and captivity on the Day of Badr, وَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ هُمُ الْأَخْسَرُونَ ** and they will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter, meaning the people who are most severe in loss (خسراناً), due to the loss of the reward (المثوبة) and the deserving of the punishment (العقوبة). (٦) وَإِنَّكَ لَتُلَقَّى الْقُرْآنَ ** (6) Indeed, you receive the Quran, that you are given it, مِنْ لَدُنْ حَكِيمٍ عَلِيمٍ ** from One who is All-wise, All-knowing, meaning, what a Wise One (حَكِيمٍ) and what a Knowing One (عَلِيمٍ). (٧) إِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِأَهْلِهِ إِنِّي آنَسْتُ ناراً سَآتِيكُمْ مِنْها بِخَبَرٍ ** (7) When Moses said to his family, 'Indeed, I perceive a fire. I will soon bring you some news from it, meaning concerning the condition of the path, because he had lost it, أَوْ آتِيكُمْ بِشِهابٍ قَبَسٍ ** or bring you a burning brand, meaning a flame of fire taken [from another source] (مقبوسة). And it was read (وقرء) with the tanwin (on شِهابٍ). The two promises (العدتان) are based on conjecture (الظنّ). For that reason, He expressed them in [Surah] Ta Ha using the form of hope (الترجّي) and alternation (التّرديد), to indicate that if he did not attain both of them, he would attain one of them, based on the apparent matter and trust in Allah, **لَعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ ** so that you may warm yourselves,' hoping that you may warm yourselves by it.

(٨) **فَلَمَّا جاءَها نُودِيَ أَنْ بُورِكَ مَنْ فِي النَّارِ ** (8) When he came to it, he was called: 'Blessed is He who is in the fire

مَنْ فِي النَّارِ (He who is in the fire) refers to those who are in the place of the fire, which is the blessed spot (البقعة المباركة) mentioned in His Almighty saying: نُودِيَ مِنْ شاطِئِ الْوادِ الْأَيْمَنِ فِي الْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبارَكَةِ ** (He was called out from the right bank of the valley, in the blessed spot), and those around it and those around its place. وَسُبْحانَ اللهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (And glory be to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds) is part of the completion of what was called out with, so that no resemblance (تشبّهاً) is imagined from hearing His speech, and for expressing wonder at the greatness of that affair (الأمر).

(٩) يا مُوسى إِنَّهُ أَنَا اللهُ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (9) O Moses! It is I who am Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. I am the Strong (القويّ), the Powerful (القادر) over that which is far removed from imaginations (الأوهام), such as turning the staff into a serpent (العصاحيّة), the Doer of everything He does with wisdom (حكمة) and planning (تدبير). (١٠) وَأَلْقِ عَصاكَ ** And cast down your staff. And he was called upon: "Cast down your staff." So when he saw it wriggling (تَهْتَزُّ), moving with agitation (تتحّرك باضطراب), as if it were a serpent (جَانٌ), a light, swift snake (حيّة خفيفة سريعة), he turned his back [on it] and fled, and did not look back (وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ). And he did not return, like the fighter (المقاتل) who returns (كرّ) after having fled (فر). يا مُوسى لا تَخَفْ ** O Moses! Do not fear. (Do not fear) out of lack of trust in Me. إِنِّي لا يَخافُ لَدَيَّ الْمُرْسَلُونَ Indeed, the apostles do not fear in My presence.**

(11) إِلَّا مَنْ ظَلَمَ ثُمَّ بَدَّلَ حُسْناً بَعْدَ سُوءٍ فَإِنِّي غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (11) except him who has done wrong, but then substitutes good for evil—then indeed I am all-forgiving, all-merciful. It is said that in it is an allusion to Moses striking the Copt. And the exception (الاستثناء) is disconnected (منقطع) or connected (متّصل). And ثُمَّ بَدَّلَ ** is a new sentence (مستأنف), conjoined (معطوف) to an omitted element (محذوف), meaning: whoever committed injustice (ظلم) then replaced his sin (ذنبه) with repentance (التّوبة). And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said] the meaning of إِلَّا مَنْ ظَلَمَ ** is 'nor whoever committed injustice' (ولا من ظلم), so a particle (حرف) was placed instead of a particle (حرف).

(١٢) وَأَدْخِلْ يَدَكَ فِي جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضاءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوءٍ ** (12) And put your hand into your bosom: it will emerge white, without any defect, affliction (آفة). In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: مِنْ غَيْرِ ** (without) leprosy (برص) فِي تِسْعِ آياتٍ ** (among nine signs), either within their totality or accompanying them, based on the fact that the nine signs are: the splitting (الفلق), the flood (الطوفان), the locusts (الجراد), the lice (القمل), the frogs (الضفادع), the blood (الدّم), the obliteration (الطّمسة), the swelling (الحدب) in their deserts (بواديهم), and the deficiency (النقصان) in their cultivated lands (مزارعهم). And for whoever excludes the staff (العصا) and the hand (اليد) from the nine, he should count the last two as one, and he should not count the splitting (الفلق) because He was not sent with it to Pharaoh. Thus it was said. إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَقَوْمِهِ إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْماً فاسِقِينَ ** (to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed they were a transgressing lot.) This is a justification (تعليل) for the sending (للارسال).

(١٣) فَلَمَّا جاءَتْهُمْ آياتُنا ** (So when Our signs came to them) by the fact that Moses came to them with them, مُبْصِرَةً ** (clear/manifest), meaning manifest (بيّنة). It is a subject participle (اسم فاعل) used for the object (المفعول), indicating that due to the extreme clarity of their manifestation to the eyes, they almost see themselves, if they were among things that see.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): that He read مبصرة with a fatḥah on the mīm and the ṣād (i.e., مَبْصَرَة), meaning a place where insight (التّبصرة) is abundant.

(١٤) قالُوا هذا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (14) They said, 'This is manifest magic.' Its magical nature is clear (واضح). وَجَحَدُوا بِها ** And they denied them And they denied them (وكذَّبوا بها), while their souls were certain of them (وَاسْتَيْقَنَتْها أَنْفُسُهُمْ). And they were certain of them wrongfully (ظُلْماً) against their own souls, وَعُلُوًّا ** (and out of arrogance) out of haughtiness (ترفّعاً) from faith (الإيمان) and submission (الانقياد). فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** So observe how was the fate of the agents of corruption. Which is drowning in this world (الدّنيا) and burning in the Hereafter (الآخرة).

(١٥) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا داوُدَ وَسُلَيْمانَ عِلْماً (And We certainly gave David and Solomon knowledge) A portion (طائفة) of knowledge, or knowledge—meaning, any knowledge (أيّ علم). وَقالا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (and they said, 'All praise belongs to Allah) Thus, they performed the act of thanking Him for what they had done. وَقالا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي فَضَّلَنا عَلى كَثِيرٍ مِنْ عِبادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (Who has preferred us to many of His faithful servants.') This includes those who were not given knowledge, or knowledge like theirs. And in this is proof of the merit (فضل) of knowledge and the nobility (شرف) of its people, since they thanked Him for the knowledge and made it the foundation (أساس) of preference (الفضل), and did not consider what they were given of the dominion (الملك)—which no one else was given—to be of importance below it. And it is an encouragement (تحريض) for the scholar (للعالم) to praise Allah for the bounty (فضله) He has bestowed upon him, and that he should be humble (يتواضع) and believe that even if he has been preferred over many, many have also been preferred over him.

(١٦) وَوَرِثَ سُلَيْمانُ داوُدَ ** (16) And Solomon inherited David. The kingdom (المُلك) and prophethood (النبوّة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): It was said to him that they speak concerning the youthfulness of his age. So he said: "Indeed, Allah revealed to David to appoint Solomon (peace be upon them both) as successor, while He was a boy tending sheep. The worshippers (عبّاد) and scholars (علماؤهم) of the Children of Israel denied this. So, He revealed to David: 'Take the staff of the speakers (المتكلّمين) and the staff of Solomon, place them in a house, and seal it with the seals (خواتيم) of the people. When the morrow comes, whoever's staff has sprouted leaves (أورقت) and borne fruit (أثمرت), he is the successor (الخليفة).' So David (peace be upon him) informed them, and they said: 'We are satisfied and we submit (سلّمنا).'"

وَقالَ يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ عُلِّمْنا مَنْطِقَ الطَّيْرِ وَأُوتِينا مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** and he said, 'O people! We have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given everything. [This was] proclaiming the favor (نعمة) of Allah, exalting it, and calling the people to affirmation (التصديق) by mentioning the miracle (المعجزة).

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man recited this Ayah in his presence, and he (peace be upon him) said: "The word مِنْ (from) is not in it. Rather, it is: وَأُوتِينا ** (and we have been given) everything. إِنَّ هذا لَهُوَ الْفَضْلُ الْمُبِينُ ** (This is indeed the manifest grace) which is not hidden from anyone."

In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means the kingdom (الملك) and prophethood (النبوّة).

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Solomon son of David was given, along with his knowledge, the knowledge of speech (المنطق) in every tongue, the knowledge of languages (اللّغات), and the speech of birds, beasts (البهائم), and predators (السّباع). When he witnessed battles (الحروب), he spoke Persian (الفارسيّة). When he sat for his governors (عمّاله), soldiers (جنوده), and the people of his kingdom (مملكته), he spoke Roman (الرّوميّة). When he was alone with his wives, he spoke Syriac (السريانية) and Nabataean (النبطيّة). When he stood in his prayer niche (محرابه) to supplicate to his Lord, He spoke Arabic (العربيّة). And when he sat for delegations (الوفود) and adversaries (الخصماء), he spoke Hebrew (العبرانيّة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from him, from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: Solomon son of David was given the kingdom of the Easts of the Earth and its Wests. He ruled for seven hundred years and six months, ruling all the inhabitants of the world: the jinn (الجنّ), mankind (الإنس), devils (الشياطين), beasts (الدوابّ), birds, and predators (السباع). He was given the knowledge of everything and the speech (منطق) of everything. And in his time, the wondrous crafts (الصنايع العجيبة) that people heard about were made. That is His saying: عُلِّمْنا مَنْطِقَ الطَّيْرِ. (We have been taught the speech of the birds.)

And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to Ibn Abbas: "Indeed, Allah taught us the speech of the birds, just as He taught Solomon son of David (peace be upon him), and the speech of every creature (دابّة) on land and sea." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, Solomon son of David (peace be upon him) said: "We have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given everything." And by Allah, we have been taught the speech of the birds and the knowledge of everything.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, the Imam is not unaware of the speech of any person, nor of a bird, nor of a beast (بهيمة), nor of anything that possesses a soul (الروح). And whoever does not possess these qualities is not an Imam."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): A pair of wood pigeons (ورشان) landed on the wall near him, and they cooed their cooing (هديلهما). He responded to their speech. They remained for an hour, then they flew away. When they flew onto the wall, the male cooed to the female for an hour, then they flew away. He (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is this bird?" He said: "Everything that Allah created—whether bird, beast (بهيمة), or anything that possesses a soul (روح)—is more attentive and obedient to us than the son of Adam. This wood pigeon suspected his wife, so she swore to him that she had not done [what he suspected]. She said: 'Are you satisfied with Muhammad ibn Ali (peace be upon him)?' So they were satisfied with me. I informed him that he was unjust to her, and he believed her."

And the narrations (الأخبار) concerning this meaning from them (peace be upon them) are numerous.

(١٧) وَحُشِرَ ** And gathered (وجمع) for Solomon his hosts from the jinn, the humans, and the birds. فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ They are held back (يحبسون). (17) And Solomon’s hosts were mobilized for him, comprising jinn, humans and birds, and they were held in check.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The first of them are held back for the last of them," meaning: so that they may catch up (ليتلاحقوا).

(١٨) **حَتَّى إِذا أَتَوْا عَلى وادِ النَّمْلِ ** (18) When they came to the Valley of the Ants,

Al-Qummi (said): He [Solomon] sat upon his throne, and the wind carried him, passing by him over the Valley of the Ants (وادي النّمل). It is a valley in which gold and silver grow (ينبت), and the ants have been entrusted with it (وكّل به). This is the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah has a valley where gold and silver grow, and Allah has protected it with the weakest of His creation, which is the ants. If the al-Bakhati camels sought it, they would not be able to reach it."

**قالَتْ نَمْلَةٌ يا أَيُّهَا النَّمْلُ ادْخُلُوا مَساكِنَكُمْ لا يَحْطِمَنَّكُمْ سُلَيْمانُ وَجُنُودُهُ وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** an ant said, 'O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Solomon and his hosts should trample on you while they are unaware.'

that they would crush you (انّهم يحطمونكم), for if they were aware (إذ لو شعروا), they would not do so (لم يفعلوا).

(١٩) فَتَبَسَّمَ ضاحِكاً مِنْ قَوْلِها (Then he smiled, laughing at her remark.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them all), regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: فَتَبَسَّمَ ضاحِكاً مِنْ قَوْلِها (Then he smiled, laughing at her remark). He said: When the ant said: يا أَيُّهَا النَّمْلُ ادْخُلُوا مَساكِنَكُمْ لا يَحْطِمَنَّكُمْ سُلَيْمانُ وَجُنُودُهُ (O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulaymān and his hosts should crush you while they are unaware.) The wind carried the ant's voice to Sulayman while he was passing through the air, carried by the wind. So he stopped and said: "Bring the ant to me." When she was brought to him, Sulayman said: "O ant, did you not know that I am a Prophet of Allah and that I do not wrong anyone?" The ant said: "Yes (بلى)." Sulayman said: "Then why did you warn them against my injustice and say: يا أَيُّهَا النَّمْلُ ادْخُلُوا مَساكِنَكُمْ (O ants! Enter your dwellings)?" The ant said: "I feared that they would look at your adornment and be tempted by it, and thus worship other than Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." Then the ant said: "Are you greater, or is your father Dawud?" Sulayman said: "Rather, my father Dawud." The ant said: "Then why was a letter added to the letters of your name over the letters of your father Dawud's name (peace be upon him)?" Sulayman said: "I have no knowledge of this." The ant said: "Because your father Dawud (peace be upon him) treated his wound with affection (بودّ), he was named Dawud. And you, O Sulayman, I hope that you will join your father." Then the ant said: "Do you know why the wind was subjugated for you from among the rest of the kingdom?" Sulayman said: "I have no knowledge of this." The ant said: "The Mighty and Majestic means by this that if the entire kingdom were subjugated for you, just as this wind was subjugated for you, its disappearance from your hands would be like the disappearance of the wind." فَتَبَسَّمَ ضاحِكاً مِنْ قَوْلِها. (Then he smiled, laughing at her remark.)

I say: Perhaps the ant, by her saying, "Because your father Dawud (peace be upon him) treated his wound with affection (بودّ)," meant that the name of your father was originally that (ذلك) but was shortened (فخفّف). She only expressed it with this phrase as an indication of the reason for the naming (علّة التسمية). Based on this interpretation, the letters of his father's name would exceed the letters of his name.

وَقالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ** (And he said: 'My Lord! Inspire me to be grateful for Your blessing) (Make me restrain (ازع) the gratitude for Your blessing that is with me, meaning: restrain it and bind it so that it does not separate from me, nor do I separate from it.)

**الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلى والِدَيَ ** (which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents,) (He included the mention of his parents therein to magnify the blessing.)

**وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صالِحاً تَرْضاهُ ** (and that I may act righteously, pleasing to You,) (To complete the gratitude and ensure the permanence of the blessing.)

**وَأَدْخِلْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِي عِبادِكَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** (and admit me, by Your mercy, among Your righteous servants!') (Among their number in Paradise.)

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Sulayman possessed the Greatest Name of Allah (اسم الله الأكبر), by which if He was asked, He granted, and if He was called upon, He answered. If it were today, he would need us.

(٢٠) وَتَفَقَّدَ الطَّيْرَ ** (20) And he reviewed the birds. And he examined the birds, but did not find the hoopoe among them. فَقالَ ما لِيَ لا أَرَى الْهُدْهُدَ أَمْ كانَ مِنَ الْغائِبِينَ** (20) and said, 'Why is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?' Al-Qummi (said): And when Sulayman (peace be upon him) sat upon his throne, all the birds that Allah had subjugated for Him would come and shade the throne and the carpet, along with everyone upon it, from the heat of the sun. Then the hoopoe was absent from among the birds, so the sun fell from its place (of shade) onto Sulayman's lap. So he raised his head and said what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related.

(٢١) لَأُعَذِّبَنَّهُ عَذاباً شَدِيداً ** (I will surely punish him with a severe punishment) like plucking his feathers, or placing him with his opposite (ضدّه) in a cage, أَوْ لَأَذْبَحَنَّهُ ** (or I will surely slaughter him) so that the members of his kind (أبناء جنسه) may take heed from him, أَوْ لَيَأْتِيَنِّي ** (or he will surely bring me) And it was read with two nūns, the first of which is fatḥahd and mushaddadah, بِسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (with a manifest authority) with a proof (حجّة) that clarifies his excuse. And the oath, in reality, is upon the first two (options), assuming the absence of the third.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): And he only became angry with him because he used to guide him to the water. He said: So this one, while being a bird, has been given what Sulayman was not given. And the wind, the ants, the jinn (الجنّ), mankind (الانس), and the rebellious devils (الشياطين المردة) were obedient to him. Yet he did not know the water beneath the air, while the bird knew it. And verily, Allah says in His Book: وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرْآناً سُيِّرَتْ بِهِ الْجِبالُ أَوْ قُطِّعَتْ بِهِ الْأَرْضُ أَوْ كُلِّمَ بِهِ الْمَوْتى ** (And if there were a Qur’an whereby the mountains could be moved, or the earth could be cut into pieces, or the dead could be spoken to) And we have inherited this Qur’an, which contains that by which the mountains are moved, the lands (البلدان) are traversed, and the dead are brought to life. And we know the water beneath the air. (The ḥadīth).

(٢٢) فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ ** (22) (But) he did not stay long. (He stayed) a time that was not prolonged, intending thereby to indicate the swiftness of his return. And it was read (وقرئ) with the dammah on the kāf (مُكِثَ). فَقَالَ أَحَطْتُ بِمَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِ ** and said, 'I have comprehended what you have not comprehended, meaning the situation of Saba'. And in his addressing him with that, there is an alert (تنبيه) that among the lowest of Allah's creation is one who has encompassed knowledge of what He (Sulayman) had not encompassed, so that he might belittle his own soul and his knowledge might seem insignificant to him. وَجِئْتُكَ مِنْ سَبَإٍ بِنَبَإٍ يَقِينٍ ** and I have brought you a certain news from Saba'. with a verified report. And Saba' was read (وقرئ سبأ) with the fathah on the hamzah (سَبَأَ) and without it (سَبَا).

(٢٣) إِنِّي وَجَدْتُ امْرَأَةً تَمْلِكُهُمْ ** (23) I found a woman ruling over them, meaning Balqis, daughter of Sharahil ibn Malik ibn Rayyan. أُوتِيَتْ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (and she has been given everything) which kings require. وَلَها عَرْشٌ عَظِيمٌ (and she has a great throne.) (٢٤) وَجَدْتُها وَقَوْمَها يَسْجُدُونَ لِلشَّمْسِ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ أَعْمالَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ** (24) I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds fair-seeming to them, and barred them from the way, [meaning] the path of truth (الحقّ) and correctness (الصّواب), so they are not guided to it. (٢٥) أَلَّا يَسْجُدُوا لِلَّهِفَصَدَّهُمْ ** (so that they do not prostrate to Allah, and barred them) [meaning] so that they do not prostrate, or زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ** (Satan made fair-seeming to them) that they should not prostrate, or they are not guided (لا يَهْتَدُونَ) to prostrate, due to the addition of لَا (not), like His saying: مَا مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ (What prevented you from not prostrating?). It was also read with the lightened أَلَّا (بالتخفيف), meaning that it is for warning (للتنبيه), and the يَا is for calling (للنّداء), with an omitted vocative (مناداه محذوف), meaning: "O people, prostrate!" الَّذِي يُخْرِجُ الْخَبْءَ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ ما تُخْفُونَ وَما تُعْلِنُونَ ** (Who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you hide and what you disclose.) This is a description of Him that necessitates His exclusive right (اختصاصه) to deserve prostration, due to His uniqueness (التفرّد) in perfect Power and Knowledge, urging prostration to Him and refuting (ردّاً) those who prostrate to other than Him. And الْخَبْءَ ** (what is hidden) is that which is concealed (ما خفي) within something else (في غيره), and its bringing forth is its manifestation (إظهاره). This encompasses the shining of the stars (اشراق الكواكب), the sending down of rains (انزال الأمطار), and the growth of plants (إنبات النبات). Rather, it includes creation (الإنشاء), for it is the bringing forth of what is in a thing potentially (بالقوّة) into actuality (الى الفعل), and origination (الإبداع), for it is the bringing forth of what is in non-existence (في العدم) into existence (إلى الوجود). It is known that this is exclusive to Allah, the Glorified. And al-Qummi said: [The hidden thing (الخبء)] in the heavens is the rain (المطر), and in the earth is the plant (النبات).

(٢٦) اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (26) Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Lord of the Great Throne. The One encompassing all creatures. (٢٧) قالَ سَنَنْظُرُ ** (27) He said, 'We shall see whether you are truthful, or one of the liars.' We shall ascertain from looking (النظر), meaning contemplation (التأمّل). أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ. (٢٨) اذْهَبْ بِكِتابِي هذا فَأَلْقِهْ إِلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ تَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** (28) 'Take this letter of mine and drop it to them, then turn away from them and see what they come back with.' Then withdraw from them to a nearby place where you can conceal yourself. فَانْظُرْ ما ذا يَرْجِعُونَ ** What they return to one another in speech. Al-Qummi said: The hoopoe said that she was in an impregnable fortress. Solomon said: Drop my letter onto her dome (قُبَّتِهَا). So the hoopoe came and dropped the letter into her lap. She was alarmed by that and gathered her troops and said to them what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related.

(٢٩) قالَتْ يا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ إِنِّي أُلْقِيَ إِلَيَّ كِتابٌ كَرِيمٌ (29) She said, ‘O assembly! Indeed, a noble letter has been delivered to me. meaning, after it was cast to her. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) (said): meaning, sealed (مختوم). And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "The nobility (كرم) of the letter is its seal (ختمه)."

(٣٠) إِنَّهُ مِنْ سُلَيْمانَ وَإِنَّهُ بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (30) It is from Solomon, and it is: ‘In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.’ This is a new sentence (استيناف), as if it was said to her, "From whom is it, and what is it?" so she said: It is (meaning the letter or the address/title) from Solomon, and it is (and that which is written is) In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

(٣١) أَلَّا تَعْلُوا عَلَيَّ وَأْتُونِي مُسْلِمِينَ ** (31) [It reads:] ‘Do not defy me, and come to me in submission.’ (meaning) believing (مؤمنين) or submitting/obedient (منقادين).

And this speech is of the utmost conciseness (الوجازة) while having complete indication (الدلالة) of the intended meaning, because it includes the Basmalah (البسملة) which indicates the Essence (ذات) of the Creator (الصانع) and His attributes (صفاته), and the prohibition of arrogance (الترفّع), which is the mother of vices (أمّ الرذائل), and the command to embrace Islam (الإسلام), which encompasses the mothers of virtues (أمّهات الفضائل). And the command therein is not for submission (الانقياد) before establishing the proof (الحجّة) of his prophethood (رسالته), such that it would be a call for blind imitation (التقليد). For indeed, the casting of the letter to her in that manner is one of the greatest proofs (الأدلّة).

(٣٢) قالَتْ يا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ أَفْتُونِي فِي أَمْرِي ** (32) She said, 'O assembly! Give me a ruling concerning my affair. Mention what you deem appropriate concerning it. ما كُنْتُ قاطِعَةً أَمْراً حَتَّى تَشْهَدُونِ ** I have not been one to decide an affair until you are present.' Except in your presence. It is as if she sought to win their affection by that, so that they might agree with her regarding the response.

(٣٣) **قالُوا نَحْنُ أُولُوا قُوَّةٍ ** (33) They said, 'We are men of strength in bodies and numbers.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Qā’im (القائم) will not emerge except among men of strength (أُولُوا قُوَّةٍ), and the men of strength (أُولُوا قُوَّةٍ) will not be less than ten thousand. وَأُولُوا بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ and men of great might. with severity and courage. وَالْأَمْرُ إِلَيْكِ The matter rests with you. is entrusted. فَانْظُرِي ما ذا تَأْمُرِينَ So consider what you command. regarding fighting or peace; we shall obey you and follow your opinion.

(٣٤) قالَتْ إِنَّ الْمُلُوكَ إِذا دَخَلُوا قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوها ** (34) She said, 'Indeed, when kings enter a town, they ruin it) by plundering wealth and destroying the homes, وَجَعَلُوا أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِها أَذِلَّةً (and make the mightiest of its people the meanest) by humiliation and captivity, وَكَذلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ. (And thus do they.) Al-Qummi said: So Allah the Almighty said: وَكَذلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ.** (And thus do they.)

(٣٥) وَإِنِّي مُرْسِلَةٌ إِلَيْهِمْ بِهَدِيَّةٍ فَناظِرَةٌ ** (35) 'I am sending them a present, and I will wait [to see] meaning: waiting (منتظرة). This is how it is in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): بِمَ يَرْجِعُ الْمُرْسَلُونَ ** what the envoys bring back.' regarding his condition, so that I may act accordingly.

Al-Qummi said: She said, "If this man is a Prophet from Allah, as he claims, then we have no power against him, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is not overcome. But I will send إِلَيْهِمْ بِهَدِيَّةٍ ** (them a present). If he is a king who inclines toward the world (الدّنيا), he will accept it, and I will know that he has no power over us." So she sent a small box (حُقَّةً) in which there was a magnificent jewel (جوهرة عظيمة). She said to the messenger: "Tell him to pierce this jewel without iron or fire." The messenger came to him with that. So Sulayman ordered some of his soldiers from among the worms (الدّيدان). It took a thread (خيطاً) in its mouth, then pierced it, and took the thread out from the other side.

(٣٦) فَلَمَّا جاءَ سُلَيْمانَ ** (When he came to Solomon) meaning: the messenger and what she gifted to him. قالَ أَتُمِدُّونَنِ بِمالٍ ** (He said, 'Do you aid me with wealth?) And it was recited with a single nūn (نون) that is emphasized (mushaddadah) due to assimilation (الإدغام). فَما آتانِيَ اللهُ ** (What Allah has given me) of prophethood (النبوّة) and kingship (الملك) upon which there is no addition, خَيْرٌ مِمَّا آتاكُمْ ** (is better than what He has given you.) So I have no need for your gift, nor does it have any significance with me. بَلْ أَنْتُمْ بِهَدِيَّتِكُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ ** (Rather, you rejoice in your gift.) Because you do not know anything except the outward appearance (ظاهراً) of the life of this world (الحياة الدّنيا).

(٣٧) ارْجِعْ ** O Messenger, return to them, meaning to Bilqis and her people, فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُمْ بِجُنُودٍ لا قِبَلَ لَهُمْ بِها (We will surely come at them with hosts they cannot resist) They have no power to resist them, nor ability to fight them, وَلَنُخْرِجَنَّهُمْ مِنْها ** (and we will surely expel them from it) from Saba, in humiliation (أَذِلَّةً), due to the departure of the glory (العزّ) they were in, وَهُمْ صاغِرُونَ ** (while they are abased.) as despised (مهانون) captives (أسراء).

In al-Qummi, the messenger returned to her (Bilqis) and informed her of that and of the strength of Sulayman. So she knew that there was no escape (محيص) for her. Thus, she departed and journeyed toward Sulayman.

(٣٨) **قالَ يا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَؤُا أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِي بِعَرْشِها قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتُونِي مُسْلِمِينَ ** (He said, 'O assembly! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?') القمّيّ When Sulayman knew of her approach towards him, he said that. It was said that he intended by that to show her some of the wonders with which Allah the Almighty had specially favored him, which indicate great power and his truthfulness in the call to prophethood, and to test her intellect by disguising her throne, so he would see whether she recognized it or denied it.

(٣٩) قالَ عِفْرِيتٌ ** (39) He said, 'An 'ifrīt (a powerful genie) (a wicked, rebellious one from the jinn) أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقامِكَ I will bring it to you before you rise from your place (your sitting place for governance/ruling. It was said that he used to sit until midday.) وَإِنِّي عَلَيْهِ ** and I am indeed, regarding it (regarding its carrying) لَقَوِيٌّ أَمِينٌ ** powerful and trustworthy.'** (I will not diminish anything from it, nor will I alter it.)

(٤٠) **قالَ الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَرْتَدَّ إِلَيْكَ طَرْفُكَ ** (40) He who had knowledge of the Book said, 'I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you.'

In al-Qummi, Sulayman said—meaning, after the statement of the ‘Ifrit (العفريت)—"I desire something faster than that." So, Asif ibn Barkhiya (آصف بن برخيا) said: أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَرْتَدَّ إِلَيْكَ طَرْفُكَ ** (I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you). Then he supplicated Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, using the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم), and the throne emerged from beneath Sulayman's chair (كرسيّ).

And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked about الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (He who had knowledge of the Book). He said: "That is the successor (وصّي) of my brother Sulayman ibn Dawud."

And in al-Basa'ir and al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: The Greatest Name (اسم الله الأعظم) consists of seventy-three letters (حرفاً). Asif only possessed one letter (حرف) of it. He spoke it, and the earth sank (فخسف) between him and the throne of Bilqis, until he took hold of the throne with his hand. Then the earth returned to its original state faster than the blink of an eye. We, ourselves, possess seventy-two letters of the Greatest Name, and one letter is with Allah, which He has kept exclusively for Himself in His knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب). And there is no might nor power except with Allah, the Most High, the Magnificent (العليّ العظيم).

And in another narration from al-Basa'ir: He spoke it, and the earth sank (فانخسفت) between him and the throne, and the two pieces (of earth) wrapped around each other, and he transferred (the throne) from this (place) to that (place).

And in another narration from al-Kafi, narrated from al-Hadi عليه السلام, he said: He spoke it, and the earth split open (فانخرقت) for him between him and Saba', so he took hold of Bilqis's throne until he transported it to Sulayman. Then the earth flattened out (انبسطت) in less than the blink of an eye.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: Indeed, the earth was folded (طويت) for him.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Hadi عليه السلام, he said: الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (He who had knowledge of the Book) is Asif ibn Barkhiya (آصف بن برخيا). Sulayman was not incapable of knowing what Asif knew, but he (peace be upon him) desired to inform the jinn and mankind that he (Asif) was the Proof (الحجّة) after him. That knowledge was part of Sulayman's knowledge, which he entrusted to Asif by the command of Allah. So, Allah made him understand that, lest there be any disagreement concerning his Imamate (إمامته) and guidance (دلالته), just as He made Sulayman (peace be upon him) understand during the lifetime of Dawud (peace be upon him), so that his Imamate and prophethood (نبوّته) after him might be known, thereby confirming the Proof (الحجّة) upon creation.

فَلَمَّا رَآهُ مُسْتَقِرًّا عِنْدَهُ قالَ هذا مِنْ فَضْلِ رَبِّي لِيَبْلُوَنِي أَأَشْكُرُ أَمْ أَكْفُرُ وَمَنْ شَكَرَ فَإِنَّما يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ رَبِّي غَنِيٌّ كَرِيمٌ (40) So when he saw it set before him, he said, 'This is by the grace of my Lord, that He may test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful, is grateful only for his own sake, and whoever is ungrateful, my Lord is indeed self-sufficient, generous.'

When he saw it—he saw the throne established (مُسْتَقِرًّا) before him, present in his hands—قالَ ** (he said), receiving the blessing with gratitude in the manner of the sincere (المخلصين) servants of Allah: هذا مِنْ فَضْلِ رَبِّي ** (This is by the grace of my Lord), a favor He bestowed upon me without my deserving it, لِيَبْلُوَنِي أَأَشْكُرُ ** (that He may test me whether I will be grateful), by seeing it as a grace from Allah, without any might or power (حول) from myself, and by fulfilling its right, أَمْ أَكْفُرُ ** (or ungrateful), by finding myself involved in the matter (في البين) or by falling short in performing its obligations. وَمَنْ شَكَرَ فَإِنَّما يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ (And whoever is grateful, is grateful only for his own sake), for by it he attracts the permanence and increase of the blessing for himself. وَمَنْ كَفَرَ (and whoever is ungrateful), فَإِنَّ رَبِّي غَنِيٌ ** (my Lord is indeed self-sufficient) regarding his gratitude, كَرِيمٌ ** (generous) by bestowing the favor upon him a second time.

(٤١) قالَ نَكِّرُوا لَها عَرْشَها ** (41) He said, 'Disguise her throne for her, by changing its form and shape نَنْظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِي أَمْ تَكُونُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ لا يَهْتَدُونَ ** that we may see whether she is guided or is one of those who are not guided.' to recognizing it. (٤٢) فَلَمَّا جاءَتْ قِيلَ أَهكَذا عَرْشُكِ ** (42) So when she came, it was said [to her], 'Is your throne like this?' to make it ambiguous for her, increasing the test of her intellect قالَتْ كَأَنَّهُ هُوَ ** She said, 'It seems to be the same.' She did not say 'It is exactly it' due to the possibility that it might be similar to it, and that was due to the perfection of her intellect وَأُوتِينَا الْعِلْمَ مِنْ قَبْلِها وَكُنَّا مُسْلِمِينَ ** [Solomon said,] 'We were given the knowledge before her, and we had submitted.' It is said that this is a continuation of her speech, as if she thought that He intended by this to test her intellect and manifest a miracle to her, so she said: وَأُوتِينَا الْعِلْمَ ** (We were given the knowledge) of the perfection of Allah's power and the soundness of your prophethood before this situation.

(٤٣) وَصَدَّها ما كانَتْ تَعْبُدُ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (43) (And what she used to worship instead of Allah had barred her) That is, وَصَدَّها ** (it barred her) — her worship of the sun — from advancing towards Islam. إِنَّها كانَتْ مِنْ قَوْمٍ كافِرِينَ ** (for she belonged to a faithless people.) And it was read (وقرئ) with the fathah on the hamzah (أَنَّها) based on al-badal (البدل), meaning صَدَّها ** (it barred her) — her upbringing (نشوها) among the midst of the disbelievers (الكفّار), or based on al-ta'lil (التعليل).

(٤٤) قِيلَ لَهَا ادْخُلِي الصَّرْحَ ** (She was told, 'Enter the palace.') The palace (القصر). And it is said: the courtyard (عرصة الدّار) of the dwelling. فَلَمَّا رَأَتْهُ حَسِبَتْهُ لُجَّةً وَكَشَفَتْ عَنْ ساقَيْها قالَ إِنَّهُ صَرْحٌ مُمَرَّدٌ ** (When she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she uncovered her shanks. He said, 'It is a palace paved smooth with glass.') That which you suppose to be water (انّ ما تظنّيه ماء). صَرْحٌ مُمَرَّدٌ ** means smoothed (مملس) مِنْ قَوارِيرَ (with glass), meaning made of glass (من الزجاج). قالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي ** (She said, 'My Lord! I have wronged myself,') By my worship of the sun (بعبادتي للشمس). And it is said: By my suspicion of Sulayman (بظنّي بسليمان), for she thought that he would drown her in the pool (اللّجة). وَأَسْلَمْتُ مَعَ سُلَيْمانَ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (and I submit with Sulayman to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds') in that which He commanded His servants (فيما امر به عباده).

It is narrated: That he (Sulayman) commanded, before her arrival, that a palace be built whose floor (صحنه) was made of white glass, and he caused water to flow beneath it, and cast sea creatures into it. He placed his throne (سريره) at the front of it (في صدره) and sat upon it. When she saw it, she thought it was stagnant water (ماءً راكداً), and وَكَشَفَتْ عَنْ ساقَيْها. (she uncovered her shanks).

And al-Qummi (narrated): He (Sulayman) had commanded that a house of glass (قوارير) be made for her, and he placed it upon the water. Then قِيلَ لَهَا ادْخُلِي الصَّرْحَ (She was told, 'Enter the palace'). She thought it was water, so she lifted her garment and exposed her shanks, and behold, there was much hair upon them (شعر كثير). So she was told: **إِنَّهُ صَرْحٌ مُمَرَّدٌ مِنْ قَوارِيرَ قالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ ** (It is a palace paved smooth with glass. She said, 'My Lord! I have wronged myself...')—the rest of the Ayah.

Then Sulayman married her, and she was Balqis bint al-Sharah al-Himyarīyah (بلقيس بنت الشراح الحميرية). Sulayman said to the devils (الشياطين): "Make something for her that will remove this hair from her." So they constructed the bathhouses (الحمّامات) and cooked the lime (النّورة). Thus, the bathhouses and the lime are among the things that the devils made for Balqis, as are the watermills (الأرحية) that turn on the water.

(٤٥) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا إِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ فَإِذا هُمْ فَرِيقانِ يَخْتَصِمُونَ. (45) We certainly sent to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ, [saying,] 'Worship Allah.' But behold, they were two contending factions.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). He said: It means a confirmer (مصدّق) and a denier (مكذّب). The disbelievers among them said: "Do you bear witness that Ṣāliḥ is a messenger sent from his Lord?" The believers said: إِنَّا ** بِالَّذِي أُرْسِلَ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ ** (We are believers in that with which He was sent). The disbelievers among them said: إِنَّا بِالَّذِي آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ كافِرُونَ وَقالُوا يا صالِحُ ائْتِنا بِآيَةٍ إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (We are disbelievers in that in which you believe, and they said: 'O Ṣāliḥ, bring us a sign if you are one of the truthful.') So, he brought them a she-camel, and they hamstrung her. And the one who hamstrung her was blue-eyed (أزرق), ruddy (احمر), and the son of fornication (ولد زنا).

(٤٦) قالَ يا قَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ الْحَسَنَةِ ** (46) He said, 'O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil before the good?) (meaning) the punishment before the repentance, for they used to say: "If His threat proves true, we will repent." Al-Qummi (narrated) that they asked him, before the she-camel came to them, to bring them a painful punishment, and they intended thereby to test him. قالَ يا قَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ الْحَسَنَةِ ** He says: the punishment before the mercy. لَوْ لا تَسْتَغْفِرُونَ اللهَ ** (Why do you not seek forgiveness of Allah) before its descent, لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ ** (so that you may receive mercy?) by its acceptance, for it will not be accepted at that time.

(٤٧) قالُوا اطَّيَّرْنا بِكَ وَبِمَنْ مَعَكَ ** (47) They said, 'We augur ill of you and those who are with you.'**

We augur ill of you, since hardships have followed one another upon us, and separation has occurred between us since you invented your religion.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): A severe famine afflicted them, so they said: "This has afflicted us due to your ill-omen (شؤم) and the ill-omen of those who are with you." This is the augury (الطّيرة).

قالَ طائِرُكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** He said, 'Your augury is with Allah.'**

He said (meaning): Your good and your evil are from Allah.

بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تُفْتَنُونَ Rather, you are a people who are being tested.

You are being tested by the succession of ease (السرّاء) and hardship (الضرّاء).

(٤٨) وَكانَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍ (48) And there were in the city nine gangs nine individuals (نَفَرٍ) يُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا يُصْلِحُونَ who caused corruption in the land and did not set things right. meaning their characteristic is pure corruption, free from any trace (شوب) of righteousness (الصلاح). Al-Qummi (said): They used to commit acts of disobedience (المعاصي) in the land. (٤٩) قالُوا (49) They said, some of them said to others: تَقاسَمُوا بِاللهِ (Swear by Allah), meaning: they made an oath (تحالفوا). This is a command (امر مقول) or a statement (خبر وقع) that occurred as a substitute (بدلاً). لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُ وَأَهْلَهُ 'Swear by Allah that we will certainly attack him and his family by night, We will certainly surprise Salih and his family by night. ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِ then we will surely say to his guardian: to the guardian of his blood (وليّ دمه). And it was recited (وقرء) as لتبيتنّه and ولتقولنّ with the tāʾ (بالتاء) and the plural form (صيغة الجمع), addressing some of them to others. ما شَهِدْنا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِ "We did not witness the destruction of his family, let alone (فضلاً) that we undertook their destruction (إهلاكهم). And [the word مَهْلِكَ] admits the meaning of the verbal noun (المصدر), the time (الزمان), and the place (المكان). And it was recited (وقرء) with the fatḥah on the lām (اللّام) along with the fatḥah on the mīm (الميم) and its ḍammah (ضمّها). وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ and we are indeed truthful." ' And we will swear: إِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ (that we are indeed truthful), or while the state (الحال) is إِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ (that we are indeed truthful). They mean: we will dissemble (نُورّي). Al-Qummi (said): He says: We will certainly do it (لنفعلنّ).

(٥٠) وَمَكَرُوا مَكْراً ** (And they devised a plot) with this arrangement, وَمَكَرْنا مَكْراً (and We devised a plot) by making it a cause for their destruction, وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ** (while they did not perceive) that.

It is narrated: that Salih had a mosque (مسجد) in al-Hijr, located in a ravine (شعب), where he used to pray. So they said: "He claims that He (Allah) will finish us off (يفرغ منّا), meaning: we will finish him and his family off before the three (days)." So they went to the ravine to kill him. Then a rock from their mountains fell upon them and sealed the mouth (فم) of the ravine upon them, so they perished there. And the rest perished in their places by the blast (الصيحة).

And in al-Qummi: So they came to Salih at night to kill him, and with Salih were angels guarding him. When they came to him, the angels fought them in the house (دار) of Salih, pelting them with stones (رجماً بالحجارة). So they awoke in his house slain, and the tremor (الرَّجْفَة) seized his people. فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ. (So they awoke in their homes fallen upon their faces).

(51) فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْناهُمْ ** (So observe how was the fate of their plotting: We destroyed them) And it was read with the fath on the hamzah وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (and their people all together.) (52) فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خاوِيَةً ** (So those are their houses, desolate) empty, derived from خوى البطن (the stomach became empty) when it is empty, or fallen and ruined, derived from خوى النجم (the star fell) when it falls بِما ظَلَمُوا ** (because of their wrongdoing) because of their wrongdoing (ظلمهم) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (There is indeed a sign in that for a people who know) so they take heed. (53) وَأَنْجَيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (And We delivered those who had faith) Salih and those who were with him وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ ** (and used to be Godwary) disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي), and for that reason, they were singled out for salvation. (54) وَلُوطاً إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ أَتَأْتُونَ الْفاحِشَةَ وَأَنْتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ ** (And Lot, when he said to his people, 'Do you commit indecency while you look on?) you know its foulness (خبثها), or some of you see it from others, and they used to commit it openly. (55) أَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجالَ شَهْوَةً مِنْ دُونِ النِّساءِ ** (Do you approach men lustfully instead of women?) who were created for that purpose. بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ ** (Indeed, you are a senseless lot.') fools (سفهاء). (56) فَما كانَ جَوابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا أَخْرِجُوا آلَ لُوطٍ مِنْ قَرْيَتِكُمْ إِنَّهُمْ أُناسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ ** (But the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Expel Lot's family from your town! They are indeed a people who keep clean.') they keep themselves far removed (يتنزّهون) from our deeds. (57) فَأَنْجَيْناهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ قَدَّرْناها مِنَ الْغابِرِينَ ** (So We delivered him and his family, except his wife, whom We determined to be among those who remained behind.) We determined her to be among those remaining in the punishment. And it was read قَدَرْناها with the lightening (تخفيف). (58) وَأَمْطَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ مَطَراً فَساءَ مَطَرُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ ** (And We rained down upon them a rain, and evil was the rain of those who were warned.) Its like has passed. (59) قُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَسَلامٌ عَلى عِبادِهِ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفى. (Say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, and peace be upon His servants whom He has chosen.') In al-Jawami', narrated from them (peace be upon them), and al-Qummi said: They are the Family of Muhammad, the blessings of Allah be upon him and upon them. آللهُ خَيْرٌ أَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (Is Allah better, or that which they associate [with Him]?) And it was read with the yā' (يُشْرِكُونَ), as an obligation (إلزام) upon them, a mockery (تهكّم) of it, and a declaration of their opinion as foolish (تسفيه).

(٦٠) أَمَّنْ ** [means] rather, who. خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَأَنْزَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَنْبَتْنا بِهِ حَدائِقَ ذاتَ بَهْجَةٍ ** He shifted from the third person (الغيبة) to the first person (التكلّم) to emphasize the exclusivity of the action to His Essence, just as He said: ما كانَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تُنْبِتُوا شَجَرَها —the trees of the gardens. أَإِلهٌ مَعَ اللهِ ** Is there anyone other than Him who is associated with Him and made a partner for Him, while He is the One unique in creation (بالخلق) and formation (والتكوين)? بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ يَعْدِلُونَ —from the truth (الحقّ), which is monotheism (التّوحيد).

(٦١) أَمَّنْ جَعَلَ الْأَرْضَ قَراراً وَجَعَلَ خِلالَها أَنْهاراً ** flowing (جَارِيَة), وَجَعَلَ لَها رَواسِيَ ** mountains (جِبَالًا) in which minerals (الْمَعَادِن) are formed and from whose depths (حَضِيضِهَا) springs (الْمَنَابِع) flow, وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ ** the sweet (الْعَذَاب) and the salty (الْمِلْح), حاجِزاً ** an isthmus (بَرْزَخًا), and its explanation has already passed in Surat al-Furqan (سُورَةِ الْفُرْقَان), أَإِلهٌ مَعَ اللهِ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** the truth (الْحَقّ), so they associate partners. (61) Is He who made the earth a resting place, and made rivers in its creases, and set firm mountains for it, and set a barrier between the two seas, is there a god besides Allah? Rather, most of them do not know.

(٦٢) أَمَّنْ يُجِيبُ الْمُضْطَرَّ ** (Or who is it that answers the distressed) whom the severity of his condition compels to seek refuge with Allah إِذا دَعاهُ وَيَكْشِفُ السُّوءَ وَيَجْعَلُكُمْ خُلَفاءَ الْأَرْضِ ** (when he invokes Him, and removes the distress, and makes you successors on the earth?) successors therein, by making you inherit its dwelling and the authority (التصرّف) over it from those who were before you. أَإِلهٌ مَعَ اللهِ ** (Is there any god besides Allah?) Who has granted you enjoyment of these blessings? قَلِيلاً ما تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (Little is it that you take admonition!) meaning, you remember His blessings (الآءه) with little remembrance. And the mā (ما) is augmentative (مزيدة). And it was recited with the doubling of the dhāl (الذّال) and with the yā' (الياء) alongside it.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: It was revealed concerning al-Qa'im (القائم) from the Household of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. By Allah, He is the distressed when He prays two rak'ahs (ركعتين) at the Station (المقام) and supplicates to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and He answers Him. وَيَكْشِفُ السُّوءَ ** and makes Him a successor on the earth. And in a narration: The first one who pledges allegiance (يبايعه) to Him will be Gabriel (جبرئيل), then the three hundred and thirteen men. And another discussion regarding this verse has preceded in Surat al-Baqarah at the Almighty's saying: أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ.** (I answer the supplicant’s call.)

(٦٣) أَمَّنْ يَهْدِيكُمْ فِي ظُلُماتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ** (63) Is He who guides you in the darkness of the land and the sea by means of the stars and the landmarks of the earth, وَمَنْ يُرْسِلُ الرِّياحَ بُشْراً بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَحْمَتِهِ and He who sends the winds as harbingers of His mercy? meaning the rain. أَإِلهٌ مَعَ اللهِ ** Is there any god besides Allah? who is capable of anything of that? تَعالَى اللهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ. Exalted is Allah above what they associate [with Him]!

(٦٤) أَمَّنْ يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ وَمَنْ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (64) Is He who originates creation, then brings it back, and who provides for you from the sky and the earth? meaning, through heavenly and earthly means (بِأَسْبَابٍ سَمَاوِيَّةٍ وَأَرْضِيَّة). أَإِلهٌ مَعَ اللهِ ** Is there a god besides Allah? who does that. Say: 'Produce your proof (بُرْهَانَكُمْ) that other than Him is capable of any of that, if you are truthful in your association (of partners) (فِي إِشْرَاكِكُمْ).'

(٦٥) قُلْ لا يَعْلَمُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ الْغَيْبَ إِلَّا اللهُ (65) Say, 'No one in the heavens and the earth knows the Unseen except Allah,'

In Nahj al-Balagha: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) once informed about some matters that had not yet occurred. So it was said to him: "O Commander of the Faithful, have you been given knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب)?" He (peace be upon him) laughed and said: "This is not knowledge of the Unseen (علم غيب); rather, it is learning from one who possesses knowledge (ذي علم)."

And the knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب) is only the knowledge of the Hour (علم السّاعة) and what Allah, the Glorified, enumerated in His saying: إِنَّ اللهَ عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ (The verse). Thus, He, the Glorified, knows what is in the wombs: male or female, ugly or beautiful, generous or stingy, wretched or happy, and who will be fuel (حطباً) for the Fire or a companion (مرافقاً) to the Prophets in the Gardens (الجنان). This is the knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب) which no one knows except Allah. And whatever is besides that is knowledge that Allah taught His Prophet, and he taught it to me, and he prayed for me that my chest would retain it and my limbs would encompass it. **وَما يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ ** (When they will be resurrected). (66) and they are not aware when they will be resurrected.'

(٦٦) بَلِ ادَّارَكَ ** (66) Nay, their knowledge concerning the Hereafter has overlapped. It followed consecutively until their knowledge of the Hereafter became firm. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) says: They knew what they had been ignorant of in the world (الدنيا). And it was read without the alif (الألف), with the dāl (الدّال) being lightened (تخفيف) and with it being doubled (تشديد). بَلْ هُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِنْها ** Nay, they are in doubt about it. In confusion (حيرة). بَلْ هُمْ مِنْها عَمُونَ ** Nay, they are blind to it. Due to the corruption (اختلال) of their insight (بصيرة). It was said that the three negations (الاضرابات) are a gradation (تنزيل) corresponding to their states.

٦٧ وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَإِذا كُنَّا تُراباً وَآباؤُنا أَإِنَّا لَمُخْرَجُونَ (67) The faithless say, 'When we and our fathers have become dust, shall we indeed be brought forth [from the graves]?' (Shall we indeed be brought forth) from the graves (الأجداث) or from annihilation (الفناء) to life (الحياة). And the repetition of the hamzah is for emphasis in the denial (الإنكار). وقرء by omitting the first (hamzah), and by omitting both of them, and (واننا) with two nūns.

(٦٨) لَقَدْ وُعِدْنا هذا نَحْنُ وَآباؤُنا مِنْ قَبْلُ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (68) 'We were promised this, we and our fathers, before. This is nothing but myths of the ancients.' Their lies (أكاذيبهم) which are like night tales (كالأسْمار). (٦٩) قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (69) Say, 'Travel in the land and observe how was the fate of the guilty.' A threat (تهديد) to them for the denial (على التكذيب) and a warning (تخويف) that the like of what descended upon the deniers (المكذّبين) before them will descend upon them. And the expression (التعبير) referring to them as the guilty (بالمجرمين) is so that it may be a kindness (لطفاً) for the guilty (للمجرمين) in abandoning crimes (في ترك الجرائم).

(٧٠) وَلا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** Do not grieve for them concerning their denial and their turning away وَلا تَكُنْ فِي ضَيْقٍ ** meaning, in tightness of the chest (حرج صدر). It was also recited with a kasrah on the ḍād (ضِيق) مِمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ ** from their scheming, for Allah will protect you from the people. وَلا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا تَكُنْ فِي ضَيْقٍ مِمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ (٧٠)** (70) Do not grieve for them, nor be distressed by what they scheme.

(٧١) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ ** They say, 'When will this promise be fulfilled,' meaning, the promised punishment (العذاب الموعود) إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (٧١)** (71) They say, 'When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?'

(٧٢) قُلْ عَسى أَنْ يَكُونَ رَدِفَ لَكُمْ ** Say, 'It may be that a part of what you seek to hasten is close behind you,' meaning, it followed you and reached you. والقمّيّ (And al-Qummi said): meaning, some of that whose occurrence you seek to hasten has drawn near from behind you. It was said that this is the punishment of the Day of Badr. قُلْ عَسى أَنْ يَكُونَ رَدِفَ لَكُمْ بَعْضُ الَّذِي تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (٧٢)** (72) Say, 'It may be that a part of what you seek to hasten is close behind you.'

(٧٣) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ ** Indeed your Lord is gracious to mankind by delaying their punishment for their acts of disobedience (المعاصي) وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَشْكُرُونَ ** they do not recognize the right of the blessing, so they do not thank Him; rather, due to their ignorance, they hasten its occurrence. وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَشْكُرُونَ (٧٣) (73) Indeed your Lord is gracious to mankind, but most of them are not grateful.

(٧٤) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَعْلَمُ ما تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ ** Indeed your Lord knows what their breasts conceal, meaning, what they hide وَما يُعْلِنُونَ ** and what they disclose of their enmity toward you, and He will recompense them for it. وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَعْلَمُ ما تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ (٧٤) (74) Indeed your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they disclose.

(٧٥) وَما مِنْ غائِبَةٍ فِي السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** There is no hidden thing in the heaven or the earth, meaning, a hidden thing in them both إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ. وَما مِنْ غائِبَةٍ فِي السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ (٧٥)** (75) There is no hidden thing in the heaven or the earth but it is in a manifest Book.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And indeed, in the Book of Allah there are signs (آيات) by which nothing is intended except that Allah permits it, along with what Allah may permit from what the predecessors (الماضون) wrote. Allah made it for us in the Mother of the Book (أمّ الكتاب). Indeed, Allah says: وَما مِنْ غائِبَةٍ ** [the rest of] the Ayah. Then He said: ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا مِنْ عِبادِنا ** (Then We made those of Our servants whom We chose heirs to the Book). So, we are those whom Allah chose and made heirs to this [Book] in which is the clarification of everything."

(٧٦) إِنَّ هذَا الْقُرْآنَ يَقُصُّ عَلى بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ أَكْثَرَ الَّذِي هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** (76) Indeed this Qur’ān recounts to the Children of Israel most of what they differ about. Like anthropomorphism (التشبيه) and transcendence (التنزيه), and the states of Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النار), and Uzayr (عزيز) and the Messiah (المسيح). (٧٧) وَإِنَّهُ لَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (77) Indeed it is a guidance and a mercy for the faithful. For they are the ones granted intercession by it. (٧٨) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِي بَيْنَهُمْ between the Children of Israel بِحُكْمِهِ meaning, by His wisdom (حكمته) or by what He judges, and that is the truth (الحقّ) وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ so His decree is not rejected, the All-knowing (الْعَلِيمُ) of the reality of what He decrees and His wisdom (حكمته). (78) Indeed your Lord will judge between them by His judgment, and He is the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (٧٩) فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ and do not care about their enmity إِنَّكَ عَلَى الْحَقِّ الْمُبِينِ and the possessor of the truth is worthy of trusting in Allah's preservation and His help. (79) So put your trust in Allah; indeed you are on a manifest truth. (٨٠) *إِنَّكَ لا تُسْمِعُ الْمَوْتى وَلا تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ الدُّعاءَ And it was recited (وقرء) with the open yā (الياء المفتوحة) and the raf' (رفع) of al-ṣumm (الصمّ) إِذا وَلَّوْا مُدْبِرِينَ They are likened to the dead and the deaf because they do not benefit from what is recited to them. (80) Indeed you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs, (٨١) وَما أَنْتَ بِهادِي الْعُمْيِ And it was recited (وقرء): tahdī al-ʿumyā (تهدي العمى) عَنْ ضَلالَتِهِمْ since guidance is not achieved except through sight إِنْ تُسْمِعُ Your making hear is not beneficial إِلَّا مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِآياتِنا who is such in Allah's knowledge فَهُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ sincere (مخلصون*). (81) nor can you guide the blind out of their error. You can make none hear except those who believe in Our signs, and thus submit.

(٨٢) وَإِذا وَقَعَ الْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (82) And when the word falls upon them which is what they were promised concerning the Return (الرجعة) upon the rising of the Mahdi (peace be upon him), as its explanation will soon follow, أَخْرَجْنا لَهُمْ دَابَّةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ تُكَلِّمُهُمْ أَنَّ النَّاسَ كانُوا بِآياتِنا لا يُوقِنُونَ ** We shall bring forth for them a creature from the earth, which shall speak to them, that the people had no faith in Our signs. And تكلمهم was recited with takhfif (lightening) from الكلم (wounding/injuring). In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Allah has wounded (كَلَمَ) whoever recited تكلمهم (with takhfif), but it is تكلّمهم (with tashdid)." And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم came upon the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) while He was sleeping in the Mosque, having gathered sand and placed His head upon it. He moved Him with His foot, then said to Him: "Arise, O Creature of the Earth (دابّة الأرض)!" A man among His companions said: "O Messenger of Allah, do we call one another by this name?" He said: "No, by Allah, it is only for Him exclusively. He is the Creature (الدّابّة) Whom Allah mentioned in His Book, when He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَإِذا وَقَعَ الْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** [the rest of] the Ayah." Then He said: "O Ali, when the End of Time comes, Allah will bring you forth in the most beautiful form, and with you will be a branding iron (ميسم) with which you will brand your enemies." A man said to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): "The common people (العامّة) say that this Creature only wounds (تكلّمهم) them." Abu Abdillah said: "May Allah wound (كَلَمَهُمُ) them in the Fire of Hell! It is only that He speaks (يُكَلِّمُهُمْ) to them, from speech (الكلام)." And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), he said: A man said to Ammar ibn Yasir: "O Abu al-Yaqzān, an Ayah in the Book of Allah has corrupted my heart and caused me doubt." He asked: "And which Ayah is that?" He replied: "His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَإِذا وَقَعَ الْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** [the rest of] the Ayah. So which Creature (دابّة) is this?" Ammar said: "By Allah, I shall not sit, nor eat, nor drink until I show Him to you." So Ammar came with the man to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) while He was eating dates and butter. He said: "O Abu al-Yaqzān, come here." So Ammar approached and sat down, eating with Him. The man was astonished by this. When Ammar stood up, the man said: "Glory be to Allah! You swore that you would not eat, nor drink, nor sit until you showed me the Creature (الدابّة)." Ammar said: "I have shown Him to you, if you possess intellect." In al-Majma', it is narrated that al-Ayyashi reported this exact story from Abu Dharr also. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said that the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "And indeed, I have been granted six things: the knowledge of deaths (المنايا), calamities (البلايا), bequests (الوصايا), and decisive speech (فصل الخطاب). And I am the master of the Returns (الكرّات) and the dominion of dominions (دولة الدول). And I am the master of the Staff (العصا), the Branding Iron (الميسم), and the Creature (الدّابة) Who speaks to mankind." In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: After mentioning the Dajjal and the one who kills him, He said: "Behold, after that will be the Greatest Calamity (الطامة الكبرى)." It was asked: "And what is that, O Commander of the Faithful?" He said: "The emergence of the Creature of the Earth (دابّة الأرض) from near al-Safa, and with Her will be the Seal of Sulayman (peace be upon him) and the Staff of Musa (peace be upon him). She places the Seal upon the face of every believer, and it is imprinted upon it: 'This is truly a believer (مؤمن حقّاً).' And She places it upon the face of every disbeliever, and it is written: 'This is truly a disbeliever (كافر حقّاً).' So much so that the believer will call out: 'Woe to you truly, O disbeliever!' And the disbeliever will call out: 'Blessedness (طوبى) is for you, O believer! I wish I had been like you, so I might achieve a great triumph.' The Creature (الدّابة) raises Her head between the two horizons (الخافقين) by the permission of Allah, majestic is His glory. That will be after the sun rises from its setting place. At that time, repentance will be lifted, and no repentance will be accepted, nor يَنْفَعُ نَفْساً إِيمانُها لَمْ تَكُنْ آمَنَتْ مِنْ قَبْلُ أَوْ كَسَبَتْ فِي إِيمانِها خَيْراً will its faith benefit a soul which has not believed before or earned some good in its faith." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Do not ask me about what will happen after this, for my beloved Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entrusted me not to inform anyone of it except my progeny (عترتي)." In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "The Creature of the Earth (دابّة الأرض) is sixty cubits long. No seeker can overtake Her, nor can any fugitive escape Her. She brands (تَسِمُ) the believer between his eyes, and 'believer (مؤمن)' is written between his eyes. And She brands the disbeliever between his eyes, and 'disbeliever (كافر)' is written between his eyes. With Her is the Staff of Musa (peace be upon him) and the Seal of Sulayman (peace be upon him). She brightens the face of the believer with the Staff and marks the nose of the disbeliever with the Seal, until it is said: 'O believer!' and 'O disbeliever!'" And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Creature (الدابّة), and He said: "By Allah, She does not have a tail (ذَنَب), but She certainly has a beard (لَلِحْيَةً)."

(٨٣) وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجاً ** (And the day We shall gather from every nation a host) meaning the Day of Return (يوم الرجعة) مِمَّنْ يُكَذِّبُ بِآياتِنا ** (from those who denied Our signs,) meaning the Imams (peace be upon them). **فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ ** (they will be held back.) their first ones will be held back for their last ones so that they may catch up with one another.

(٨٤) حَتَّى إِذا جاؤُ ** (When they come,) to the gathering place (المحشر). قالَ أَكَذَّبْتُمْ بِآياتِي وَلَمْ تُحِيطُوا بِها عِلْماً أَمَّا ذا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (He will say, 'Did you deny My signs without comprehending them in [your] knowledge, or what was it that you used to do?') or what thing كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (you used to do) after that? And this is for reproach (للتّبكيت), since they did nothing other than denial (التكذيب).

(85) وَوَقَعَ الْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ (And the sentence fell upon them) The promised punishment befell them بِما ظَلَمُوا (due to their wrongdoing, which is the denial of Allah's signs) فَهُمْ لا يَنْطِقُونَ (so they will not speak) with an excuse, due to their preoccupation with the punishment.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the hadith—which was previously mentioned in the interpretation of al-Dābbah (الدّابة)—He (peace be upon him) said: The proof that this refers to al-Rajʿah (الرجعة) (the Return) is His saying: وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجاً (And the day We shall muster from every nation a host) He (peace be upon him) said: The signs (الآيات) are the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (peace be upon them). The man said: "The general populace (العامّة) claims that His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجاً refers to the Day of Resurrection." He (peace be upon him) replied: "Would Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, muster only a host from every nation on the Day of Resurrection and leave the rest? No. Rather, it refers to al-Rajʿah (الرجعة). As for the verse concerning the Resurrection, it is: وَحَشَرْناهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً (and We shall muster them, and shall not leave out anyone of them) [18:47]."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): There is no believer who was killed but that he will return until he dies (a natural death), and none will return except those whose faith (الإيمان) was pure (محضاً) and those whose disbelief (الكفر) was pure (محضاً).

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: بَعَثْنا عَلَيْكُمْ عِباداً لَنا أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ (We sent against you servants of Ours possessing great might) He (peace be upon him) said: They are a people whom Allah sends forth before the advent of al-Qā’im (القائم), and they will not leave any avenger for the family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) but that they kill him. The complete hadith has already preceded in Sūrat Banī Isrā’īl, so we have no need to repeat it.

He said in al-Majmaʿ: Narrations have been abundant from the Imams of Guidance from the family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) that Allah the Almighty will bring back, upon the rising of al-Mahdī (peace be upon him), a group of those who died previously from among His friends (أوليائه) and followers (شيعته), so that they may attain the reward of supporting and assisting Him, and rejoice in the manifestation of His state. And He will also bring back a group of His enemies so that He may take vengeance upon them, and they may receive some of the punishment they deserve, either by being killed at the hands of His followers or by the humiliation and disgrace they witness from the ascendancy of His word. No rational person doubts that this is within the power of Allah the Almighty and is not inherently impossible. Allah has indeed done this among past nations, and the Quran speaks of it in several places, such as the story of ʿUzayr (عزيز) and others, as we have explained in its proper place. It is authentically narrated from the Prophet (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him and his family) that He said: "Everything that happened among the Children of Israel will happen among My Ummah, exactly like one sandal matches the other, and one feather matches the other, so much so that if one of them entered a lizard's hole, you would enter it too."

I say: Al-Hasan ibn Sulaymān al-Hillī (may his resting place be pleasant) compiled a book on the virtues of the People of the House (peace be upon them), in which he cited many narrations proving al-Rajʿah (الرجعة) and detailing its circumstances. In it, he mentioned that al-Dābbah (الدابّة) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), according to many narrations whose meanings concur. He transmitted most of these from the book of Saʿd ibn ʿAbd Allāh, titled Mukhtaṣar al-Baṣā’ir (مختصر البصائر). Let us cite here one hadith from his book; whoever desires the rest should refer to it. It is what he narrated from Al-Aṣbagh ibn Nubātah: ʿAbd Allāh al-Kawwā’ al-Yashkurī stood up before the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, some people among your companions claim that they will return after death." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Yes. Speak only what you heard, and do not add to the words you spoke to them." He said: "I said, 'I do not believe in anything you have said.'" The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "Woe unto you! Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, tested a people because of their sins, so He caused them to die before the appointed terms set for them, then He returned them to the world so that they might fulfill their provisions (أرزاقهم), and then He caused them to die after that."

He said: So ʿAlī ibn (1) al-Kawwā’ exclaimed Allāhu Akbar (كبّر) but did not grasp it. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "Woe unto you! Do you know that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said in His Book: وَاخْتارَ مُوسى قَوْمَهُ سَبْعِينَ رَجُلاً لِمِيقاتِنا (And Moses chose seventy men from his people for Our tryst) So he went with them so that they might bear witness for him, when they returned to the assembly of the Children of Israel, that My Lord had spoken to Me. If they had submitted to that and believed it, it would have been better for them. But they said to Moses: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً (We will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly) Allah the Almighty said: فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ (then the thunderbolt seized you)—meaning death— وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ ثُمَّ بَعَثْناكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (while you looked on. Then We raised you up after your death, so that you might be grateful) [2:55-56]. Do you see, O son of al-Kawwā’, that these people returned to their homes after they had died?" Ibn al-Kawwā’ said: "And what is that? Then He caused them to die in their place?" The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "Woe unto you! Has He not informed you in His Book where He says: وَظَلَّلْنا عَلَيْكُمُ الْغَمامَ وَأَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَنَّ وَالسَّلْوى (And We shaded you with clouds, and We sent down to you the manna and the quails) This was after death, when He resurrected them."

"And another example for them, O son of al-Kawwā’, is the assembly of the Children of Israel, where Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ وَهُمْ أُلُوفٌ حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ فَقالَ لَهُمُ اللهُ مُوتُوا ثُمَّ أَحْياهُمْ (Have you not regarded those who left their homes, and they were thousands, for the fear of death? Thereupon Allah said to them, ‘Die,’ then He revived them) [2:243]. And His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, concerning ʿUzayr (عُزير), where Allah informed [us] and said: أَوْ كَالَّذِي مَرَّ عَلى قَرْيَةٍ وَهِيَ خاوِيَةٌ عَلى عُرُوشِها قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها فَأَماتَهُ اللهُ (Or like him who passed by a town, while it lay fallen upon its roofs. He said, ‘How will Allah revive this after its death?’ Thereupon Allah caused him to die) [2:259]. And He seized him for that sin مِائَةَ عامٍ ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُ (for a hundred years, then He raised him up) [2:259] and returned him to the world. Then He said: كَمْ لَبِثْتَ (‘How long have you stayed?’) [2:259]. He replied: لَبِثْتُ يَوْماً أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ قالَ بَلْ لَبِثْتَ مِائَةَ عامٍ (‘I have stayed a day or part of a day.’ He said, ‘No, you have stayed a hundred years’) [2:259]. So, do not doubt the power of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, O son of al-Kawwā’."

(٨٦) أَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ لِيَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ ** (Do they not see that We have made the night for them to rest in it) with sleep and repose (القرار).

وَالنَّهارَ مُبْصِراً (and the day to see). It was said that its origin is لِيُبْصِرُوا فِيهِ (that they may see in it), so rhetorical emphasis (مبالغة) was achieved therein by making sight (الأبصار) a state among its inherent states (أحواله المجبول عليها).

إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ. (There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith.)

وَيَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ فَفَزِعَ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَنْ شاءَ اللهُ وَكُلٌّ أَتَوْهُ داخِرِينَ (٨٧) (87) And the day the Trumpet is blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified, except whomever Allah wishes, and all will come to Him abased.

(It means) in the horn (القرن). It is narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked about it, and He said: "It is a horn (قرن) made of light, which Isrāfīl has taken into his mouth (التقمه)." So, it is described by wideness (السعة) and narrowness (الضيق). There is a difference of opinion as to whether its top is narrow and its bottom is wide, or the reverse, and there is a justification (وجه) for each view. And it is reported that in it there are holes (ثقباً), a hole (ثقبة) for every human being, containing his soul (روحه). and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified due to the horror (الهول). And He expressed it using the past tense (الماضي) because its occurrence is certain (لتحقّق وقوعه). except whomever Allah wishes that he should not be terrified, by making his heart firm (يثبت قلبه). and all will come to Him abased. meaning humbled (صاغرين). And it was read with the shortening of the hamzah (قصر الهمزة) and the opening (فتح) of the tā’.

footnote (١) In the copies (*النسخ*) that we possess, it reads: "Then ‘Alī ibn al-Kawwā’ said *Allāhu Akbar*." It is apparent that it is ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Kawwā’, as is not hidden.

(88) وَتَرَى الْجِبالَ تَحْسَبُها جامِدَةً ** (And you see the mountains, which you suppose to be stationary) fixed in their place وَهِيَ تَمُرُّ مَرَّ السَّحابِ ** (while they will be moving like the movement of the clouds.) in swiftness. And that is because when large bodies (أجرام الكبار) move in a single direction (سمت واحد), their movement is scarcely discernible (لا تكاد تتبيّن حركتها). صُنْعَ اللهِ الَّذِي أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ ** (The making of Allah, Who has perfected everything.) He perfected His creation and fashioned it as it ought to be. إِنَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَفْعَلُونَ ** (Indeed He is well aware of what you do.) He is knowledgeable of the apparent and hidden aspects of deeds, and He will recompense them for them. And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتّاء).

(٨٩) مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها وَهُمْ مِنْ فَزَعٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ آمِنُونَ (89) Whoever brings virtue shall receive better than it, and they will be secure from the great terror on that day. And it was recited with al-idhafah (بالإضافة). (٩٠) وَمَنْ جاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَكُبَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِي النَّارِ (90) But whoever brings vice—their faces will be hurled into the Fire. So they will be hurled into it upon their faces: هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (Are you requited except for what you used to do?)—with the intention of the saying (القول). Al-Qummi said: The virtue (الحسنة), by Allah, is the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and the vice (السيئة), by Allah, is following His enemies. And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), regarding this verse, He said: The virtue (الحسنة) is the recognition of the wilayah (الولاية) and the love of us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), and the vice (السيئة) is the denial of the wilayah (الولاية) and the hatred of us, the People of the House (أهل البيت). Then He recited the verse. And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَمَنْ يَقْتَرِفْ حَسَنَةً نَزِدْ لَهُ فِيها حُسْناً (And whoever performs a good deed, We shall enhance its goodness for him).
