He said: "Whoever takes as his guardian (تولّى) the Successors (الأوصياء) from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and follows their traces, that increases his wilayah (ولاية) of those who passed away among the Prophets and the first believers, until their wilayah (ولاية) reaches Adam (peace be upon him). And that is the saying of Allah: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها (Whoever brings virtue shall receive better than it)—We shall admit him into Paradise." And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding this, He said: The virtue (الحسنة) is the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali and his love, and the vice (السيئة) is enmity towards him and hatred of him, and no deed is accepted (لا يرفع) alongside them. A narration concerning the beginning of these two verses has already passed at the end of Surat al-An'am.

(٩١) إِنَّما أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ رَبَّ هذِهِ الْبَلْدَةِ الَّذِي حَرَّمَها ** (91) I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this town, who has made it sacred. Al-Qummi said: meaning Mecca (مكّة), which Allah the Almighty has honored. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Quraysh demolished the Ka'bah, they found in its foundations a stone with an inscription that they could not read well, until they called a man who read it. Behold, it contained: "I am Allah, the Lord of Bakkah (بَكَّة). I made it sacred the day I created the heavens and the earth, and I placed it between these two mountains, and I surrounded it with seven angels, surrounding it." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arrived in Mecca (مكّة) on the day He conquered it, the door of the Ka'bah was opened, and He commanded that the images (صور) inside the Ka'bah be erased. Then He took hold of the door jambs and said: "Behold, Allah has made Mecca (مكّة) sacred the day He created the heavens and the earth. Thus, it is sacred by the sacredness of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, until the Day of Resurrection. Its game shall not be startled, its trees shall not be cut down, its grass shall not be gathered, and its lost property (لقطتها) is not lawful except for one who announces it." Al-Abbas said: "O Messenger of Allah, except for idhkhir (إذخر), for it is used for graves and houses." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Except for idhkhir (الإذخر)." وَلَهُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ ** in creation (خلقاً) and ownership (ملكاً). وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** the submissive ones (المنقادين).

(٩٢) وَأَنْ أَتْلُوَا الْقُرْآنَ ** (and that I recite the Quran) And that I persist in its recitation so that its realities may be unveiled to me gradually (شَيْئاً فَشَيْئاً) during its recitation. فَمَنِ اهْتَدى ** (So whoever is guided) by following me in that, فَإِنَّما يَهْتَدِي لِنَفْسِهِ ** (he is guided only for his own sake) for its benefits return to him. And whoever goes astray by opposing me, فَقُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُنْذِرِينَ ** (say, 'I am only one of the warners.') then nothing of the burden (وَبَال) of his misguidance falls upon me, since the Messenger is only responsible for the conveyance (الْبَلَاغ), and I have conveyed [the message].

(٩٣) وَقُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ** (93) And say, 'All praise belongs to Allah.' on the blessing of prophethood, and on what my Lord taught me and enabled me to act upon it. سَيُرِيكُمْ آياتِهِ ** (He will show you His signs) when you return to the world and they return, فَتَعْرِفُونَها ** (and you will recognize them) so you will recognize that they are the signs of Allah when recognition will not benefit you. Al-Qummi said: The signs (الآيات) are the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them). When they return to the world, their enemies will recognize them when they see them in the world. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, Allah has no sign (آية) greater than me." وَما رَبُّكَ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ and your Lord is not oblivious of what you do so do not assume that the delay of your punishment is due to oblivion of your deeds. And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياء). And the reward for reciting the three Ṭawāsīn (الطّواسين) has already passed.
